hints.text: Plate white (passenger), **yellow = commercial** (unique in SE Asia; Laos yellow = regular); drives **left** (KH/VN/LA drive right)
**Thai script**: compact, with tiny circles inside most letters (Khmer = longer lines/accents; Lao similar → check driving side)
Bollard = **square with a pointed top**, black-and-white stripes (also KH/LA/ID)
**Concrete roads** with a large square-slab pattern common (also Philippines); **yellow middle lines** (Malaysia = white)
Square poles with small **vertical holes**; street-light poles striped red/black or black/white at the base
Very distinctive **long thin lamp posts** overhanging the road; square bulky white signposts w/ black-painted bottom
Directional signs: small roads white (2-part, **Garuda** emblem if no place name); highways green w/ Latin below Thai
Distinctive **green village signs** (found in no other country); many street flags incl. yellow royal flags
Most **Gen3 has no visible car**; Thai-script stop signs (3 chars; Lao = 2)
National road **first digit = region**: 1 = north, 2 = NE, 3 = centre, 4 = south; Bangkok phone code 02
Provincial waystones + **black boxes** carry a 2–3 letter **province abbreviation** (Thai script) → pinpoint province
**Muslim south**: Jawi (Arabic-based) script on signs, mosques, Islamic attire
Tall mountains → NW; low forested mtns → centre-north; flat & dry → N/E of Bangkok; **red soil** → NE
**Bangkok pink & yellow-on-green taxis**; red **songthaew** pickups → Chiang Mai; Phuket = touristy, hilly, resorts
